description: Create a content slide with a three-column scenario table on China's near-term economic outlook and a right-hand sidebar bullet list of what to watch for.

header.headline: China is expected to sustain growth near its 5% policy target by doubling down on exports, but risks are tilted to downside due to industrial overcapacity and lingering property woes
header.sub: China near-term economic outlook and key scenarios

rows: Growth dips below 4% as property correction persists, consumer confidence stays weak, and policy support has limited traction; Deflationary pressures linger and demand remains uneven
Growth holds at around 4.5% on the back of continued export momentum in EVs/energy/high tech; Export market share gains offset tariff headwinds and weak domestic demand
Growth of >5% as productivity boost from AI/automation and revival of consumer demand complement ongoing export strength
Slow property and LGFV cleanup; Household savings stay high and consumption recovery stalls; Softening global demand and more trade barriers vis-a-vis China
Greater policy support and scaling of capacity in high-tech and advanced manufacturing; Tariff headwinds partly absorbed through price cuts, supply chain shifts, and EM market expansion
Property market stabilization and targeted household support lift near-term consumer demand; Faster innovation in AI, automation, green tech boosts activity
Weak demand and margin pressure from overcapacity; Rising corporate default
Widening gap in domestic vs. foreign demand; Tougher global pricing
Uplift in consumer-facing sectors; Productivity gains support improved pricing power

items.sub: Degree of continued strength in export categories (e.g., EVs, batteries, solar modules, advanced machinery) and any new tariff/anti-dumping actions from US/EU
Improved inventory absorption, completions, funding for unfinished projects, and restructuring progress for large developers and LGFVs

items.text: Scale and effectiveness of policy stimulus
items.sub: Responsiveness of domestic demand to fiscal support (consumption incentives, social-housing programs, tax tweaks) and PBoC credit guidance
Price and capacity developments in key sectors will signal whether supply continues to outpace global absorption and the effectiveness of anti-involution policies

items.text: Balance of global vs. China-led supply chain shifts
items.sub: How quickly/extensively foreign firms accelerate reshoring and “China-plus-one” strategies vs. Chinese manufacturers expanding offshore assembly and EM localization to sustain export momentum
